questions.question: Jake and his brother buy snacks. Jake buys 3 burgers at \$4 each and a drink for \$2. He has \$6 left. His brother buys a pizza for \$12 and 4 bags of chips. If his brother started with twice as much money as Jake and spent all his money, how much does one bag of chips cost?
questions.explanation: Jake spent \$14 and had \$6 left, so he started with \$20. Brother started with \$40. After buying a \$12 pizza, he had \$28 for 4 bags of chips. \$28 ÷ 4 = \$7 per bag.

questions.question: A cyclist rides 3 miles per hour faster than her training partner. The cyclist finishes a 20-mile route in 2 hours. How fast does her training partner ride?
questions.explanation: Cyclist speed = 20 miles ÷ 2 hours = 10 mph. Partner rides 3 mph slower: 10 - 3 = 7 mph.

questions.question: Elena tutors for \$18 per hour. This week, she worked twice as many hours as last week. Her total pay for both weeks is \$432. How many hours did she work last week?
questions.explanation: Let $h$ = hours last week. This week = $2h$. Total: $18(h + 2h) = 432 \rightarrow 54h = 432 \rightarrow h = 8$.

questions.question: A train travels 15 miles per hour faster than a bus. The train covers 180 miles in the same time the bus covers 120 miles. What is the speed of the train?
Priya sells candles. Small candles sell for \$6 and large for \$10. She sold three times as many small candles as large ones. Her total sales were \$224. How many large candles did she sell?
Adrian is 5 years older than his sister. In 3 years, Adrian will be twice as old as his sister was 2 years ago. How old is Adrian now?
A teacher buys pencils and erasers. Pencils cost \$0.50 each and erasers cost \$0.75 each. She buys a total of 40 items and spends \$26. How many pencils did she buy?
questions.explanation: $p + e = 40$, $0.50p + 0.75e = 26$. Multiply by 4: $2p + 3e = 104$. Substitute $e = 40 - p$: $2p + 120 - 3p = 104 \rightarrow p = 16$.

questions.question: A delivery driver earns \$50 per day plus \$3 per package delivered. On Monday she delivered twice as many packages as on Tuesday. Her total earnings for the two days were \$172. How many packages did she deliver on Tuesday?

A pool fills at a rate of 12 gallons per minute. After 10 minutes, a drain opens and water leaks out at 4 gallons per minute. If the pool holds 600 gallons when full, how many minutes total until the pool is full?
questions.explanation: First 10 min: 120 gallons. Remaining: 480 gallons. Net rate: 8 gpm. Time: 60 min. Total: 10 + 60 = 70 minutes.

questions.question: A movie theater charges \$8 for adults and \$5 for children. On Saturday, the theater sold 150 tickets and collected \$1,020. How many children's tickets were sold?

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You can translate complex real-world situations into equations and solve them step by step.
scoreMessages.70: Good work. Focus on: 1) Clearly defining variables, 2) Setting up equations based on relationships described in the problem, 3) Solving systems using substitution.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. For rate problems, use distance = rate × time. For mixture problems, set up one equation for total quantity and one for total value.
scoreMessages.0: Start by: 1) Reading the problem carefully and identifying what you need to find, 2) Assigning a variable to the unknown, 3) Writing equations that model the situation, 4) Solving and checking that your answer makes sense.
